data.title: Alphabet Stock's Price Target Lowered at Wells Fargo Ahead of Q2 Earnings – Here's Why
data.description:
With the third quarter now underway, attention is shifting toward the upcoming Q2 earnings season. Naturally, Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) readout will draw plenty of attention; the search giant is set to release its quarterly report on July 22.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The Street will be keeping a close watch on the performance of Alphabet's cloud business, which delivered a 63% revenue increase to $20 billion in Q1, as well as ongoing AI-driven improvements in Search advertising.

Ahead of the print, Wells Fargo analyst Ken Gawrelski thinks that "healthy core trends" offer reasons for optimism even amid rising investment levels. That said, his FY27 and FY28 EPS estimates are reduced by 4% following several adjustments, including capital raises, revisions to cloud backlog assumptions, and the impact of a new capacity agreement.

That also affects Gawrelski's price target, which goes from $435 to $416, implying the shares will now gain 15% in the months ahead. Gawrelski's Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating stays as is. (To watch Gawrelski's track record, click here)

Nevertheless, Gawrelski expects strong results both for cloud and search.

He forecasts Q2 cloud revenue growth of 72% year-over-year vs. the Street at 65%, supported by continued capacity-driven momentum and sustained incremental margins of around 50%. However, he also makes adjustments to backlog assumptions that could reduce FY27 and FY28 cloud revenue and operating income forecasts, even though they remain meaningfully above consensus.

Gawrelski also anticipates external TPU sales becoming a more "meaningful contributor" in the second half, likely starting in Q3, with those sales helping drive an acceleration in second-half revenue growth to around 150%, although incremental margins are expected to ease into the low 30% range.  

On Search, the analyst expects Q2 revenue growth of 17% YoY, which he sees as better than feared for a second consecutive quarter, driven by "accelerating search session growth." Gawrelski notes that search engagement "continues to accelerate," as use cases expand, reducing near-term pressure on AI monetization.

Gawrelski does not expect "additional quantitative guidance" on FY27 CapEx, where his estimate sits at $290 billion vs. a street estimate of roughly $250 billion, although he believes market expectations are now closer to $300 billion to $350 billion. He also has not yet incorporated potential AI supply chain inflation into either capital expenditure or cloud revenue forecasts, but expects "upward pressure" on both. "We believe hyperscalers will be able to pass through supply chain inflation to customers and preserve attractive returns, but the market needs reassurance from the big 3 cloud providers," the analyst said.

Finally, he expects the new cloud capacity agreement, modelled at a $2.76 billion quarterly run rate beginning in Q4, to be used for Gemini model training and booked within R&D and Alphabet-level activities. Gawrelski also does not expect this agreement, priced at "well-above market rates" of an estimated $50 billion per gigawatt, to be repeated.

Turning now to the broader Street view, where the stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 28 Buys vs. 5 Holds. The average price target lands at $428.12, offering 12-month returns of 18.5%. (See GOOGL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Stock Futures Inch Higher; Investors Await June Jobs Report
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Thursday morning as investors awaited the June jobs report, which will provide key insights into U.S. employment and influence the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.15%, 0.11%, and 0.13%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EDT on July 2.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Oil prices trended lower amid easing tensions in the Middle East. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 1.7% to $70.35 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell 1.92% to $67.23.

During Wednesday's regular session, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.66% while the S&P 500 slipped 0.22% due to sharp declines in chip stocks. Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) plunged more than 10%, while Intel (INTC) fell over 9%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones briefly hit a new intraday record before ending little changed.

Meanwhile, Palantir (PLTR) stock was up 3% in Thursday's pre-market trading after D.A. Davidson upgraded the AI-powered data analytics company to Buy from Hold, citing an attractive valuation. Also, AeroVironment (AVAV) stock was up 4% after the defense technology company won a $500 million contract with the U.S. Army to develop counter-drone capabilities.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Should Investors Buy AMD Stock after the Selloff? Here's What Wall Street Thinks
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell nearly 7% on Tuesday after a report said Meta Platforms (META) may begin selling excess AI computing power. The news raised concerns that Meta could buy fewer AI chips in the future. Even so, Wall Street believes the recent pullback does little to change AMD's long-term outlook. Several analysts have recently raised their price targets, saying demand for AMD's AI chips and server processors remains strong. Notably, AMD stock has gained over 150% so far this year.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Why D.A. Davidson Still Backs AMD after the Selloff

In a new report, D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria said reports that Meta may sell excess AI computing power could create a near-term risk for AMD. If Meta already has more computing power than it needs, the company may buy fewer AI chips in the future.

Even so, Luria kept his Buy rating on AMD. He believes Nvidia (NVDA) remains the top choice for AI chips, but AMD should continue to benefit as customers look for alternatives. Luria also said demand for AI computing still exceeds supply, meaning the long-term outlook for the sector remains strong.

Other Analysts Remain Bullish

Luria is not the only analyst who remains positive on AMD.  Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers recently raised his price target on AMD to $615 from $505 and kept an Overweight rating. He expects strong demand for AMD's EPYC server processors and AI chips as cloud companies continue expanding their AI data centers. Rakers also believes AMD can generate more than $20 in annual earnings per share sooner than previously expected.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Cathie Wood Sells Alibaba Stock (BABA). Should Investors Be Concerned?
data.description:
Cathie Wood continues to trim her Alibaba (BABA) stake, but should investors be concerned? Not according to Wall Street. After selling about $20 million worth of Alibaba stock over the past week, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest sold another 79,632 shares worth roughly $7.8 million on July 1. Even so, analysts continue to rate the stock a Strong Buy. The average price target suggests nearly 99% upside, even as the shares remain down about 33% this year.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Why Wall Street Still Likes Alibaba

Alibaba continues to face several near-term challenges. Consumer spending in China remains weak, while the company is investing heavily in AI and cloud infrastructure, putting pressure on margins. Geopolitical tensions and regulatory uncertainty have also weighed on investor sentiment this year.

Even so, Wall Street remains bullish on the stock. They believe the recent weakness does not change Alibaba's long-term story. Daiwa analyst John Choi recently kept a Buy rating on the stock, even after lowering his price target. He continues to rank Alibaba as his top China e-commerce pick, pointing to faster cloud growth and improving profitability. He also believes Alibaba's AI business offers strong long-term growth.

Jefferies also remains bullish on Alibaba. The firm recently named it its "top China internet pick," saying Alibaba Cloud remains one of the company's biggest strengths. Jefferies believes the cloud business and the company's growing AI services position Alibaba well to benefit as more businesses adopt AI.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy?

According to TipRanks, Alibaba has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 unanimous Buy ratings assigned over the past three months. The average BABA stock price target of $194.94 implies about 99% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: White House Races to Finalize AI Model Rules With OpenAI, Google and Anthropic
data.description:
The White House is speeding up advanced talks with top artificial intelligence firms in the U.S. to create voluntary rules for testing new AI models before they launch. The proposed framework is being developed with leading AI companies like ChatGPT creator OpenAI, tech giant Google (GOOGL), and Claude AI developer Anthropic.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The standards, scheduled to be released as early as next week, are expected to shape how future frontier AI models are tested, reviewed, and deployed.

White House Pushes U.S. AI Firms Toward Shared Safety Rules 

According to reports, the White House's proposed rules would establish proper benchmarks for advanced AI models and create clearer timelines for their testing and launch. The framework is also expected to define who can access the most powerful AI systems in the U.S. and abroad.

The push follows an executive order signed by President Donald Trump in June. The order directs federal agencies to work with leading AI developers to test advanced models before public launch. The order also called for the development of industry-wide rules to address any security risks.

One person familiar with the talks at the White House said the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) and the National Security Agency (NSA) is expected to play a key role in setting and monitoring the new rules.

Notably, technical teams from the AI firms have met consistently with White House officials over the past week to reach a final agreement. Negotiations have reportedly focused on review timelines and the threshold at which an AI system is considered a "frontier" model.

Recent AI Restrictions Add Urgency to White House Talks

The U.S.'s effort to conclude the AI rules comes after a series of government actions involving some of the industry's most advanced models. Earlier this month, the U.S. Commerce Department lifted export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. 

The ban was terminated less than three weeks after the White House cut access to the AI models due to national security concerns. OpenAI has also faced similar resistance. Last week, the company delayed the full public release of GPT-5.6 at the request of the U.S. government. 

According to people familiar with the matter, Google is also in discussions with the government. The tech giant plans to release advanced coding models with stronger cyber capabilities than earlier versions. 

Which AI Stocks are a Strong Buy?

While Anthropic and OpenAI remain private companies with no publicly traded stock yet, investors interested in AI stocks can explore established players like Google (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT). Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks currently rate all three a Strong Buy. (See Their Stock Forecasts)
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Nvidia's (NVDA) AI Chip Financing Strategy Raises Risk Questions
data.description:
Nvidia Corporation (NVDA), the leading chipmaker, is using its balance sheet to help more companies buy its high-priced AI chips. The move could support demand for Nvidia's hardware, but it also raises new questions about how much financial risk the company is taking on to keep the AI boom moving.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

AMZO: built for a short position on AMZN

According to The Information, Nvidia is offering financial backstops to young cloud providers that rent out its GPUs. Under the program, Nvidia would agree to rent back unused GPU capacity if these companies cannot find enough AI developers to use it. In return, Nvidia could receive a share of the cloud providers' revenue.

The effort is reportedly known inside Nvidia as the AI Compute Partnership. An Nvidia spokesperson confirmed the program, according to the report.

Meanwhile, NVDA shares dropped 1.25% on Wednesday, closing at $197.58.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: The Macro Case for Staying Neutral on Nvidia (NVDA)
data.description:
The macro case for staying neutral on Nvidia (NVDA) comes down to one question: can today's artificial intelligence (AI) spending and infrastructure buildout remain strong enough to justify the stock's premium valuation? Nvidia is still the dominant winner in the AI power boom, but I'm approaching the company through a more sensitive macro lens rather than simply praising its execution. While the market is well aware of how strong AI demand is, hyperscaler capex, power availability, financing conditions, and customer returns have to be durable to support today's premium valuation.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

The accelerated computing and AI leader still has a lot going for it in the future, including AI factories, inference, networking, and Rubin. However, all it takes for a de-rating is the market pricing in an AI infrastructure boom as cyclical capex; that can happen even before real earnings compress. That's why I'm staying neutral on NVDA for now.
data.author: Oliver Rodzianko

data.title: Meta Stock Defies Pushback on WhatsApp Username Rollout in Its Biggest Market
data.description:
Shares in social media giant Meta (META) were green early Thursday despite reports that the Indian government opposes its planned rollout of usernames on WhatsApp. India is the American company's largest market for the messaging app.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

On Monday, Meta announced that the three billion WhatsApp users worldwide could register to reserve a unique username for their accounts. This marked a departure from using phone numbers as the platform's primary identifier.

Meta said the move was aimed at improving users' privacy, including when they join group chats. The company is expected to implement the change later this year.

India Opposes WhatsApp Username Launch

However, in a letter dated July 1 seen by Reuters, the Indian government expressed worry that the new feature could lead to significantly more harmful misuse. It cited online fraud, phishing, and impersonation as examples.

As a result, the letter said Meta must pause the rollout until it has consulted with the government. The document also notes that Meta has three days to respond to the government's concerns about the planned change.

Of the more than three billion people on WhatsApp, over 500 million users are from India. This means that India alone accounts for about 16.7% of Meta's WhatsApp market.

Meta Races to Monetize WhatsApp, Other Platforms

Meanwhile, the letter comes as early reports indicate that public figures might have problems reserving their names. This raises the risk of scams.

For instance, Changpeng Zhao, the former CEO of Binance and one of the most prominent names in the crypto world, was unable to reserve his preferred username. Meta, in its announcement, had said it would allow creators and organizations to secure their existing Instagram or Facebook handles for WhatsApp.

Meanwhile, the move comes as Meta has been trying to boost user engagement and monetize its platforms. WhatsApp is its largest messaging asset. In May, the tech giant launched subscription plans for the app, as well as Instagram and Facebook.

Is Meta Stock a Buy or Sell Right Now?

On Wall Street, analysts remain highly bullish on Meta's shares and have a Strong Buy consensus rating. This is based on 31 Buys and five Holds issued over the past three months.

In addition, the average META price target of $818.81 implies about 34% upside in the months ahead.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
